channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Nintendo Criminally Undervalued — 88 replies — 352.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo Criminally Undervalued

Open thread • Replies: 88 • Updated: 2026-05-12 20:43 UTC

OP

Honestly believe Nintendo is grossly undervalued. Considering a buy at current levels. Thing is, I’ve never bought an ADR before. What are the advantages/disadvantages of buying an ADR

Early replies (first 3)

62212154
>>62212149
Not buying that bing bing wahoo slop. Sorry.

62212158
>>62212149
I tried this when it dropped to 16. Stopped out at 12. I would let it bleed for a while might go lower.


About ADRS… beats buying on the tokyo exchange. Its 100 shares minimum on tokyo. Adr you can buy any amount. Only disadvantage is lower liquidity. If you want the actual shares use ibkr for tokyo, otherwise NTDOY is fine.

62212161
>>62212149
Nintendo was trading around 3 dollars during the wii u saga. It can easily drop another 50% from here.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62213646
i went to see a palm reader and she told me that in 2030 nintendo will fuse with valve to release their games on PC, effectively ending console gaming and all alternatives to steam.

62213657
>>62213569
I'd buy at the 5 range

62213925
Crashing 12%
LMFAO LOL

62213949
>below LINK
Ohhhhhh Nintendieeeeeeeeeees

62213977
>>62213235
>You are aware that shares can go to zero right?
>"I don't mind it going to zero"
Do you know how to read? More upside doesn't mean stock will bounce up and down. It means the reward ratio of it being 0 is close to impossible due to it being backed by Tencent, having enough equity to pay off the debt, having IP's 4 times worth more than the debt.

>>62213213
>Gamers are so fucking stupid it's unreal. Ubisoft is a dying company, 9/10 of their projects don't make enough money
Wow what thoughtful response. Except it's not 9/10. It's been the last 4 games due to them being complacent with selling 7/10 games which made them a lot of money for a time. The entire company has restructured, they've gotten old game devs, quality controller much stricter. You can easily see it in AC BF Resynced and majority of the game was still made under the old team. Also, Tencent isn't going to let their 800m (and additional 1 billion on IP) dollar investment just go into flames. Ubisoft made a deal with the board (including Tencent) to not be takeover by Tencent for 8 years in 2022 to allow for a turnaround. This deadline is in 4 years. Tencent will want to fully take over the company by 2030 and have every incentive to not allow the debters to take their investment and IP.

62213982
>>62213925
Nintendos Market cap is below Activision when it got bought out for 69Billion. I doubt Nintendo isn't worth 55B.

62214016
Gaming is dead. All new games are something redone and done again, remakes and remasters, nostalgia bait and whatever. A lot of old IP are being killed by woke shit. A lot of people are just buying emulation consoles and playing old games. The last console generation with a huge amount of good games was ps2 (just for example: gta3, gta vc, gta sa). After that, the games that people are playing like crazy are the ones that people are addicted for decades like cs and dota.

62214017
>>62214016
Games that have online multiplayer with micro transactions, that's why on ps2 we had 3 gta, but gta v had 3 playstations.

62214026
>>62212149
Not buying your bing bing wahoo. Simple as

62214263
>>62212192
>ubislop
Im sorry to hear that

62214411
>>62212203
How do you think the stat of a gorillion % of stockpickers underperform arose.

62215019
>>62212149
bing bing wahoo slop top

62215031
https://www.zerohedge.com/markets/memory-crunch-sends-nintendo-shares-deeper-bear-market

I told you so and don't run away from your public shaming OP.

62215101
>>62214263
Pretty sure Guillemot has ties to the French government. It'll be ok.

62215398
>>62215031
You don’t base your investment decisions off zerohedge slop, do you?

62215752
>>62215398
I used to, and yea they lost me a lot of money the damn Bulgarian Russian fucks.

62215887
>>62212149
>undervalued
How, why? Explain this without analysing price but the actual valuation of the company.

62215912
>>62213519
Lmao I can feel the desperation thought your post.
Next time buy s&p500 fag.

62215923
>>62215912
>Through your post
Damn spell checker

62215940
>>62215887
Nintendo is the cultural pillar of japanese economy.

62215953
I don't fully get it.
Nintendo makes most of its money from software sales right? That side is hardly affected by hw issues.
They might lose profits on console sales and the worst thing being diminished install base growth which affects the game sales.
Have they really been that overvalued?
They are not going anywhere and their games will continue selling millions.

62216426
>>62213213
It's why we see sony getting into cars. When IMO they need to get into appliances since the margins are better.

62217444
>>62213320
Yes, but so far Nintendo is going full hollywood and theme parks, which isn't ideal. They should go harder on other shit for the stock to go higher, Switch 2 sales are already in a decline.
>>62213977
>Except it's not 9/10. It's been the last 4 games due to them being complacent with selling 7/10 games which made them a lot of money for a time
>Last 4 games
Look at the list here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Ubisoft_games:_2020%E2%80%93present
I want you to look at the list here and dare if any of these are successful besides maybe AC Shadows, which isn't a smash hit as they would've wanted to get out of Tencent. If your only successful product line is from the AC series and nothing else, then you're looking at a company that's in the brink of ruin. Tencent will wait for Ubisoft to keep shooting itself in the foot until they get every worthwhile IP of theirs for cheap. I'm not saying this for you by the way, I'm saying this for everyone else not to be retarded and buy Ubisoft stocks.
>>62216426
Appliances are hard when they have stiff competition from Korea and China. Sony owns Music labels, animation studios and various many things but they still need to downsize to become more profitable. Their phones are non-existent, they're making joint operations with TCL on TVs now. If anything they need to look at Panasonic and just downsize and focus on extremely profitable industries and not bother with anything else.

62217766
>>62212192
>unironically Ubisoft
zamn

62219639
>>62217444
Kino reply. Got any more info on Sony?

62219641
Nintendo neglects their core business.
It's fine to really want to make money on licensing. But it's not fine to let the game's unit languish. They deserve to be beaten down.

62219725
Zoomers and Alphas aren`t buy trash like Milenials manchild.

Their games run better on android emulator than their own videogame.

62219799
>>62212612
Nintendo only owns a third of The Pokemon Company.

62221370
>>62219725
Good morning saar

62222931
>>62212192
the first half of your post is gold info
the 2nd half is poo poo

item.title: >redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo… — 27 replies — 108.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD >everyone here is poo…

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-12 20:49 UTC

OP

>redditors are getting rich and posting their 1M dollar screenshots from investing in RKLB and AMD
>everyone here is poor as fuck

Early replies (first 3)

62208450
just goes to show 4chan is much more retarded than >leddit :^)

62208458
only a few more weeks until I can post on reddit

62208475
>>62208412
We were targeted specifically by kikes to be made poor

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208495
>>62208412
i've been pointing out how reddit outperforms /biz/ for years and nothing has changed here

62208497
>>62208495
How would you even begin to prove such a baseless assertion? You think redditors invented dollar cost averaging, portfolio diversity, or having optimism about tech industries?

62208501
>>62208497
no way to prove it, i have simply observed the average frequency of gains and reddit is the clear winner

/biz/ however won with the OG LINK marines

62208528
>>62208412
>>everyone here is poor as fuck
Everyone here is the most retarded kind of skeptic, bagholder fudding new investments, or bear who thinks the market is going back to 30k on the DJIA for nk reason.

62208530
>>62208501
>>62208497
>>62208475
Reddit doesn't ruthlessly purge The Next Big thing until it's too late to make sizable gains, it doesn't have low IQ browns phoneposting "SAAR BUY THE TOP OF A MASSIVE CANDLE" it doesn't have 50yo xoomers fudding every good investment becuase "muh tech bubble was bad" 20 years ago.

62208534
>>62208528
kek you are poor

62208537
>>62208458
my ip is range banned from reddit permanently

62208538
Don't kid yourselves. Crypto is in the beginnings of a decade long bear market. /pmg/fags will get the final laugh.

62208544
>>62208528
you keep taking my screenshot. I want to strangle you

62208568
>>62208530
Reddit does, in fact, purge a lot of its comments. It is also filled to the brim with botposting. Don't know why you'd treat their circlejerk as more valuable than any other random shitpost you read online.

>>62208544
I should take it and use it as mine too.

62208833
>>62208412
I see WSB getting filthy rich meanwhile /biz/ chuds are here bagholding chainlink and "investing" in rocks

62209921
>>62208412
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62209936
>>62208501
the weird part here is we have bears that never post ANY gains LOL

62211249
>>62208537
based
>>62209921
go post that over there

62213463
>>62208412
I'm doing ok

62217703
>>62208412
why is this thread still up its been 2 days wtf

62218664
>>62209921
>take a $1,000,000 loan

Suddenly a millionaire.

62218683
>>62213463
give me 100k and you'll fix my life

62218698
>>62208530
I remember the dot com crash
>/b/ told me pets.com the make it stock

62218792
>>62208458
So what will you do with the money once it reaches 1 million, will it be enough for you?

I wont be satisfied until I reach at least 3 million which is 10-12 years away at a minimum.

62218804
>>62208530
reddit has loser bears too

62221909
if I were to sell everything today I'd have 20K€

62222773
>>62221909
What were you doing for the past 10 years? if you saved 25-30k a year youd have at least over 400k by now from the compounded interest.

62222950
>>62218792
I already have over a million. I am an oldfag already, so I am aiming for 2.5 or so before I consider retiring. with retirement accounts and cash I am around 1.6. realistically if I retire early my biggest cost will be health insurance. I don’t want to risk going without in my late 40s and 50s.

item.title: So DTCC pinned a tweet today — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
So DTCC pinned a tweet today

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-12 20:44 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62220461
>DTCC
dumb trannies collecting chainlink
>pinned
like a donkey? like an ass? kinda gay
>tweet
rhymes with feet, kind of fucking weird
>today
article says q4 2026, they just wanted to post this to make it seem like the clarity draft is a big deal

62220505
>>62220461
this and based pilled

62220534
>>62220448
And ofc BTC dumps. The real reason of why BTC would keep dumping until october its gonna be massive news from link. Fun fact IS that link Will dump too

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62220545
>>62220534
It sucks but the only way link re-enters the top 5 is if btc goes to 25k, and link holds strong at $5+.

62220558
i only have 6k link will i make it

62220571
>>62220558
1k+ link will eventually make it, just depends on how long you are willing to wait

62220605
>>62220571
I will wait forever…

62220615
>>62220448
>first thought: i bet LINK is negative on the news lol
>check
>mfw it's actually down 2%

62220625
>>62220615
Your first though should've been "I bet Bitcoin is negative on the news".

62220685
we won

62220696
>>62220685

62220700
>>62220448
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOM BULLISH LINK

62220813
>>62220685
That's not the right one

62220846
>>62220625
His first thought acted was “how can I please big Serg with my mouth and anus?”

62221109
they turned off the comments on you moonboi annoying fucks and xrp's fetish with you kek lmao

62221114
>>62221109
DTCC comments been disabled forever

62221160
>>62221114
could you just....you know

62221184
XRP boys are fuming LOL

62222481
>>62220448
>announced
>progress
>toward
>goal
>advancing
kek just another decade or two and we'll get there

62222581
>>62220615
This time was actually different, it mooned up 1% in the ten minutes after the dtcc put out the tweet with a big volume spike included before the market did it’s thing

62222933
>>62220685
>>62220696
Rent free

item.title: RLC: Digital Dogshit — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
RLC: Digital Dogshit

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-12 20:36 UTC

OP

Anyone else still holding onto their oil?

Day of the barrel soon right? Right?

Early replies (first 3)

62214651
>soon
Literally exactly at this time (may) in 2021 when it pumped from like 1.50 in april to 20 bucks in one massive weekly candle.

62215272
>>62214623
I boughted more...

62215293
>>62215272
Gilles thanks you

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62221834
>>62215293
I'm disappointed with the pivot from compute to privacy, that worker pools never manifested.

62221944
>>62214623
I'm just about still alive and held onto my oil

62222009
>>62214623
no i sold that shit like two years ago. bought AMD with it

62222850
>>62214623
> Biggest ICO, get 100s of millions dollars worth of BTC / ETH.
> Starts with public computer, gets no traction
> I know, we need industry regulated version! Has literally 0 purchases and needs to beg holders to buy some. Quietly kills the idea with zero usage
> Pivots to Oracles too late, misses the pump
> Pivots to AI Agents, can't be bothered to update their demo since 2017, misses the pump
> Pivots on private compute, refuses to add GPU support even though thats what everyone wants. Gets mogged by render despite the head start
> Pivots to privacy infra, their big play is a confidential email. Everyone says wtf why not just use a burner? Misses the privacy pump
> Middle of the biggest compute demand in history. Decides to double down on privacy infra and misses the pump

Is this the most retarded team to have ever existed?

62222898
>>62214623
Which one of you is the guy who bought BAT, lost money, then dumped it all for RLC? Lmao

item.title: XMR — 14 replies — 26.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
XMR

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-12 20:19 UTC

OP

Delisted at 160, currently at 410

Early replies (first 3)

62213668
Nobody gives two shits about crapto in general. You are a baggie trying to find a greater fool, you were the greatest fool

62213696
>>62213668
You are cattle.

62213702
>>62213696
No, I am cashflow that is never going to have a demand for your bags. No matter what you baggies advertise

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214009
>>62213668
I made it through XMR, it's an excellent opportunity for anyone who listens.

62216189
I like the coin

62216387
>>62214009
Same I dumped on newbaggies when we hit 800. Years of accoomoolating paid off.

62216481
>>62213665
Yeah but what can I buy with it? Where do I sell it for fiat?

62217118
>>62216481
Check monerica, you can buy pretty much anything.

62217182
>>62216481
>Yeah but what can I buy with it?
Anything. I'm currently buying work supplies for my business with XMR. That compounded with how much I've made my business is basically free and I'm making a couple thousand dollars a week.

62222692
>>62214009
based
sound money, safe mode

62222705
>>62217182
based
I've been shilling it in Lebanon, and trying to educate everyone
fuck central banks
fuck fiat
(((keynesians))) deserve the rope

https://www.france24.com/en/live-news/20210601-lebanon-crisis-among-world-s-worst-since-1850s-world-bank

never forget

62222769
>>62222705
Glad to see you're still around

62222781
>>62222769
thank you my friend
we have a saying here, roughly translates to:
if God is with us, who can be against us

62222813
>>62222781
Lovely, could've come straight from the apodeipnon

item.title: Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k)… — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k)…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-12 20:46 UTC

OP

Do I give up WFH for 50 percent raise and a job that will be good for my career or do I stay WFH for poverty wage(100k) and focus on finishing my masters and not huffing fumes in traffic?


I feel like i wont regret giving up wfh later in life but I'll be miserable for the next 3 years.. I feel like im fucked either way.

Early replies (first 3)

62222340
>>62222336
Meant to say later in life like 3 years later when I quit cuz I cant go in the office my while life, id rather be dead. But it might be worth it to suck it up for a few years

62222349
What's the masters in? This is the hinge the decision should be on.

62222450
>>62222336
Consider how much money you would spend on gas commuting everyday for a year, and consider that gas could still go up another 50% from today's prices. So you know that you are spending $3.5k — 5.2k in gas this year citing today's average American gas price for calculations. Is office life worth this sacrifice?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62222880
>>62222336
take the 50% raise wtf

62222939
how long is the gommute

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-12 20:40 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62222823
>>62219625
my most successful strategies don't care about high winrate, they just win big on a few and cut losses early on the rest. so in that case, less trades = less chances to win big.

62222917
>>62219625
>Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right?
you'll actually get a smoother equity curve if you take more trades but lower risk-per-trade. if you trade less you'll have more luck-based variance in your equity curve.

on win-rate & RR: 50% win rate and 1:1RR really isnt good. its much easier to increase to 2:1RR than it is to try to control your win-rate over large amounts of trades.

i think your best path would be to choose the top 1-3 strategies you have and either increase your RR or try to get your win-rate towards 70% in a scalping style.

item.title: Trump's billionaire entourage combined wealth exceeds GDP of most countries — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Trump's billionaire entourage combined wealth exceeds GDP of most countries

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-12 20:37 UTC

OP

62222789
so we basically have a post-soviet oligarchy now

62222791
based, survival of the fittest type shi

62222832
>>62222766
In this current world, that is absolute power. Not nukes. This.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62222900
>>62222766
And Musk is 93% of that "entourage" wealth lmao. You've got fucking Ironman and everyone else is hawkeye.

item.title: Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo… — 304 replies — 33.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Cerebras IPO May 14th edition >Educational sites: >Financial TV Streams: >Charts: >Screeners: so you feel like in the mo…

Open thread • Replies: 304 • Updated: 2026-05-12 11:44 UTC

OP

Cerebras IPO May 14th edition

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

>NEW THREAD: Copy-paste the following when making a new thread:
https://pastebin.com/NyzbAsX4

PREVIOUS
https://boards.4chan.org/biz/thread/62217769/smg-stock-market-general#top

Early replies (first 3)

62218691
> futures

62218697
AVGO, SITM, DVN & GIS
WY to 30 eventually

62218700
INTC ain’t stopping until 2T in market cap. Believe it (dattebayo)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62219778
I'll call the AI industry a bubble when its over 100 trillion and theres not a dyson swarm, and theres not robots everywhere, and human life hasnt been extended 100+ years from healthcare breakthroughs

62219788
ITS OVER
ITS GOING TO BE A BLOOD BATH
AAAAAAAAAAAAA

62219789
>>62219778
So what will you call it when it's back to 50 million and theres not a dyson swarm, and theres not robots everywhere, and human life hasnt been extended 100+ years from healthcare breakthroughs?

62219795
>>62219746
this is unironically bigger, much bigger disruptive scale than just the average goyim getting access to email and online travel booking. dotcom displaced travel agents and newspaper classifieds, maybe a million jobs total in the US over a decade. agents are coming for the entire white collar middle class, 60 million plus US workers doing the kind of repetitive cognitive labor that frontier LLMs are specifically good at.

62219820
>>62218848
$820k
40
SW engineer

62219835
>>62219564
I'm charging it out at as a variation with a floating average for larger clients.
And an inflated fixed cost average for smaller clients.

It took a lot of negotiations initially but the reality set in to developers.
My diesel costs per month went from $30k to nearly $80k. I have staff vehicles as well as machines

62219848
>>62219835
>>62219570
for

62219849
>>62218685
Anyone buying this IPO?

I don't want to be a goyim.

62219854
I have an important question

With ibkr

can you buy fractional shares with Japanese stocks? By fractional, I mean, can you buy 1 share? It's hard for me to hit my exact balance with Japanese stocks when I have to buy 100 lot shares. I know ibkr used to require you to buy 100x lots of Japanese shares. I just wonder if they let you buy 1 shares, instead of forcing 100. Its hard to weight this shit when 100 shares of some JP companies is $50,000 lol

62219860
>>62219854
No.
The asian Stock Exchanges have minimums

62219876
>>62219860
Are you sure? Because I checked online, and some sources said yes, and others no

Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?

62219878
>>62219860
And by the way, it doesnt matter if the asian stock exchanges require minimums if the broker does fractional shares. I want to know if IBKR specifically does fractional shares for JP stocks.

62219882
>>62219854
If you're a Euro, use Trading 212

62219884
>>62219876
>Did you try to buy 1 JP shares on IBKR in the last year or less?
How about right now?

62219889
now the market wants to dip

62219892
>>62219882
>>62219860
>>62219884
But Gemini says yes

62219894
>>62219889

62219897
>>62219892
>But Gemini says yes
Why are you asking AI?
And why are you asking us?
You can either do it or you can't, and you can't.

62219906
>>62219884
Check your settings in trading permissions. See if you have fractional shares enabled.

62219909
>>62219906
No, you.

62219914
>>62219909
Please

62219922
>>62219909
If you do it, Ill tell you what JP tickers I want to buy

62219931
>>62219922
You need an account registered as "expert trader" to unlock the option.
So if you want to try it out, you'll have to lie and claim you're an expert with over 10 years experience

62219938
>>62219931
>He isn't an expert with over 10 years experience

NGMI

62219986
>>62219931
why wont you just lie?

62220010
>>62219986
Because an investor only has so many lies in a day and he needs to save them for explaining to the wife whete all their money went.

62220032
>>62220010
thats bullshit
but I believe it

62220034
>>62219986
Because I told the truth about my experience when I opened the account. Now I have to make up some lie to my broker how I instantly gained 8 years experience to show an anonymous frog whether he can buy fractional anime shares.

62220186
why are prediction markets legal? It's literally gambling, and zero sum

item.title: Two positive hantavirus cases in the US. — 24 replies — 12.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Two positive hantavirus cases in the US.

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-12 18:57 UTC

OP

Two positive hantavirus cases in the US.

Early replies (first 3)

62214336
>>62214321
you live in squalor

62214342
>>62214314
>>62214321
>>62214336

If it's positive what's the big deal?

62214345
>>62214314
Just sold 100k, thanks!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62214347
>(((chatgpt))) trying to downplay the real threat to goyim

62214418
>>62214314
mva-hanta vax ;)

62214439
>>62214314
Those are rookie numbers, you gotta bump those numbers up to bring down oil prices.

62214464
>>62214321
>we're veterans of losing the "don't eat rat feces while having unprotected gay sex" challenge, greetings.

62214673
>>62214314
Oh... Oh my fucking god. I just reentered the market.

62214681
>>62214673
you know india's stock market just crashed and the "trump insider" with 100% win record opened a short for tomorrow morning?

62214716
>>62214314
Its already priced in

62214727
>>62214314
hyperbullish PFE

62214862
>>62214314
there is only one confirmed case and its in a lab for studying. Nice try though. People are so retarded for selling. lol

https://www.foxnews.com/health/american-evacuated-cruise-ship-tests-positive-hantavirus-another-symptoms-hhs-confirms

62214899
>>62214314
All the fatties in the chud states about to die

62214905
>hentaivirus
what did they mean by this?

62214952
>>62214321
Some people are just born mentally ill. Being in a first world country doesn’t protect from that.

62215045
>>62214314
>he doesn't know
Hantavirus is endemic in the US, and has been as long as humans have been here. Gene Hackman's wife died of Hantavirus last year. It's a fucking nothingburger.

62215060
>>62214336
Miguel Squalor

62215111
>>62214681
American short? Pic?

62215944
>>62214314
Aint that shit extremely difficult to get?

62218252
guys a hantavirus just flew over my house

62219214
>>62214314
What is the correlation between a positive test and a supposed symptom? Do symptomatic people test positive more often than asymptomatic people? In which percentage?
Does the test have a gold standard, something that it is compared to, like it is supposed to be done, and if yes, what is it?
Is it a PCR test? If yes, did they isolate the supposed virus sequence, with a control experiment?
Is it an antigenic test? If yes, did they test the positivity being exclusively positive with an isolated virus (with control experiment), and nothing else?

62219216
>>62215944
What disease is extremely easy to get? I mean done in real conditions, with control experiment?

62219218
>>62219216
>What disease is extremely easy to get?
HIV if you're down to clown ;)

62219270
>>62214314
Pfizer and Netflix buy signal?

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 84 replies — 11.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 84 • Updated: 2026-05-12 13:34 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62208168
>>62208095
I was asking why he would sell at 120k....

If it reaches ATH again, it's going to go higher.

62209247
>>62205030
Good choice anon. Never really liked the whole wrapped BTC meta either.

62209253
>>62208030
Yeah and still think the space matures eventually once better infrastructure and security become the priority.

62209260
>>62208168
Are you also holding?

62209263
>>62207947
Yeah true, but can’t really generalize everything either. Some protocols got hacked for completely different reasons too.

62209287
>>62208072
days over? You're lying

62209291
>>62209260
Hodl

62209304
>>62195275
BTC dominance still looks too strong for a full alt run right now.

62209376
>>62207965
Let's see how it goes

62209379
>>62204588
So are you into btc?

62209385
Bitcoin is getting fucked

62209525
>>62209385
Utility coins barely move unless the market gets euphoric again. Just btc

62209590
People pretending fundamentals matter in crypto will never stop being funny.

62209854
>>62195275
Everyone becomes a macro analyst after holding one red candle lmao

62210047
>>62209304
but when?

62210050
>>62209291
hold and stake

62211194
>>62207855
I've got some hbar in my portfolio

62211429
>>62211194
hbar going down to 2c before it ever gets a chance at real traction

62211440
>>62195275
>Chainlink is the future

62211870
>>62211440
Hahaha

62211903
>>62195275
Everyone moved to BTC of course, I dumped my ETH for BTC too, I used to hold BTC because of staking but now I can stake BTC natively thanks to TBV and the likes

62211915
>>62195275
I’d rather keep stacking BTC, then put it to work via native staking / yield layers (like Babylon-style setups) instead of chasing dead charts and hoping for a narrative pump.

62211928
>>62209385
Ok as you say so

62211932
>>62211429
Hbar is okay to hold and buy

62212034
>>62195275
More potential upside on alts for the next six months. Specifically memes right now.

62213245
>>62211440
What's your price target anon?

62214696
>>62211932
All in or?

62216973
>>62211915
It's worth considering anon

62216987
>>62195275

Polkadot

62220595
>>62209854
Are you sure?

item.title: Saylor bought another 2,500 BTC today — 19 replies — 10.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saylor bought another 2,500 BTC today

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-05-12 19:01 UTC

OP

How does he keep doing it?

He's offering 11% interest, and using the money to buy thousands of BTC.
This is going to send BTC to 1m a coin and he's going to be the richest man on the planet the first trillionaire.

Early replies (first 3)

62217727
Saylor is running an elaborate Ponzi scheme. This joker is scaring potential buyers, nobody wants some dot com bubble washout to be sole holder and buyer of a finite asset.

>t. 0.35 bitcoin holder

62217744
>>62217727
It's not a ponzi because Saylor is filing everything with the SEC and his trading volume is just a drop in the bucket of the rest of the global bitcoin industry.

I think he just found a way to accelerate the price and adoption by giving boomers 11% returns while he makes 30%+ buying BTC.

62217831
>>62217744
It’s a Ponzi in the way that if people stop buying MSTR or STRC, it’ll all collapse and Saylor will need to sell bitcoin. But I’ll be totally real with you, there’s a very small but real chance that this is the start of bitcoin banking as described by Hal Finney. This could be pulled off, or it could all collapse in a very entertaining manner.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62218197
>>62217744
It's a ponzi, even if it's technically legal.

They generate no cash, the only way he's paying the interest / dividends is from new buyers. Simple as that.

62218218
He's buying BTC because he knows very good AI in a couple years will anally rape credit card and banking software and people will be forced to use crypto.

62218280
>>62218218
>Duuuude AI bro
>Agentic AI will have to use crypto bro
>Why does it have to be BTC specifically?
>Well... it just does okay?!

62218382
Owning that much bitcoin unironically has no utility, Saylor's 61 Billion dollar reserve is at the mercy of Bitcoin miners (total hardware value: $5.42 billion in 2026 [made in China]) which refuse to adopt a quantum road map due to their rigs getting bricked. Unironically bitcoin has way more utility outside of its native chain, a Defi user can deposit their tokenized bitcoins on defi right now and get a stablecoin loan at 4% (ex: aave) so they can buy more bitcoin, thus outperforming the Saylor's 11%.

62218416
>>62218382
I view saylor as an existential risk to BTC at this point (price wise). If Strategy goes tits up it will be ugly.

62218843
The reality is MSTR is mostly neutral/mildly positive to bitcoin price. Newfags see 11% apy and happily buy STRC over buying bitcoin. Then strategy turns around, buys the bitcoin, puts it on their balance and issues common stock to pay the preferred. The result is MSTR mcap goes up, share price stays relatively flat and bitcoin goes up a little but doesn't go down as much because it's held institutionally and not by someone emotional faggot. MSTR probably ends up being JPM in a decade or two but it's going to be very boring until then.

62218859
What if Saylor eventually buys almost all Bitcoins (ticker: BTC) and demands collapses cause what use could some currency only one guy has have?

62218868
>>62217744
11% interest is nuts. you better hope btc goes up +11% a year or its a loss.

62218877
>>62218218
Yea bro AI bad actors will target banks and credit cards backed by militaries over crypto and DeFi protocols backed by shitty code sitting in a 3rd world server room. I mean, it's not like North Korea has been nonstop raping every crypto protocol under the sun for the past year

62221794
>>62218868
Strategy has enough billions set aside to pay that 11.5% for years regardless of BTC price movements.

62221837
>>62217744
>his trading volume is just a drop in the bucket
his trading volume is basically the only authentic buy volume in the bitcoin market. right now the "bitcoin market" consists of saylor paypigging for large bitcoin holders (i.e. the "crypto cabal") who obtained their stacks for pennies on the dollar and who are happy to offload whenever he shows up with the next pallet of cash.

62221895
>>62218843
You think Saylor can print 1.5 billion dollars worth of MSTR stock every year for 10-20 years?

62221908
saylor always buy the top

62222202
>>62217708
He can see the writing on the wall for the USD.
If you can't see it after this debacle in Iran, then there's no hope for you.

62222404
Why buy STRC when you can buy SATA? It pays 13% APR and its backed by STRC anyway

62222419
>>62217708
imagine if he sent all of it to a burn address

item.title: High IQ thread — 14 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
High IQ thread

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-12 19:09 UTC

OP

When did Capitalism die exactly? Prices keep rising for no reason, yet the quality of materials seems to get cheaper and cheaper. Planned obsolescence. Endless subscriptions. Taxes on taxes. Compulsory insurance. Nothing of value is being created. Even movies and art are stale and mediocre at best, despite (((billions))) in production budgets. When did it all just... end? Pls don't reply "the moment I nutted inside yuor mom" let's keep the conversation respectful

Early replies (first 3)

62221100
>>62221092
the same reason monarchism dies. eventually you get a string of bad kings and the whole thing implodes. if the people running the show are retarded misanthropes, the state of civilization will reflect that.

62221121
>>62221092
>When did Capitalism die exactly?
wdym it’s working like intended. It was never supposed to create value, just corporate profit.

62221347
>>62221092
Absurd real taxes. Including VAT and everything else its 65% in Germany and i guess around 50% is the usa. Then the state is wasting it. 150 years ago it was maybe 5%. This sucking in of wealth broke capitalism.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62221413
>>62221092
That is all caused by fiat.

62221437
>>62221092
>OP requests "high IQ thread", implying he has a high IQ
>writes paragraph about inflation
>"prices keep rising for no reason"
>does not understand that the government simply increasing the money supply is what's causing this phenomenon
>mfw OP is a retard and has no place in this "High IQ" thread
I'll post ITT OP if you promise not to post in it :)
PS. Study Austrian economics. Read either Murray Rothbard or Saifedean Ammous (for a modern summary)

62221586
>>62221092
When you grew up. Now delete your thread. I'm sick of boomer nostalgia man.

Also what do you mean by "art"? Do you attend photo galleries or something? As for movies, you would be making the same frogpost 75 years ago over Westerns and Musicals so please just shut up man.

62221753
>>62221586
I think movies topped in the 2000s.

62221787
The red pill is that war was the arbiter of wealth redistribution.
By ending war between the great powers, their elites are permanently entrenched. There's no winners, no losers, no wealth transfer. They're free to accumulate off of compound interest and then rent seek to an ever higher degree.
Since the Rothschild's won the last wars they're here until there's another real war to get rid of them.

62222243
>>62221121
Correct. And really it is working pretty well, you're just a megagrumbler pretending like everything is shitty. If you want something good you can get it, and you enjoy a standard of living far beyond any generation. You have more clothes than ten people would have 100 years ago, computing power 100 times that which got us to the moon in your pocket, AC, fridge, food available year round people would see never, etc etc.

If you're mad at fiat currency, neat. Otherwise you're mad that people generally would prefer to spend $20 for a pair of pants than $500

62222410
>>62221092
when the gold standard was abandoned

62222412
>>62221092

Shut up

62222424
>>62221092
Yes, we've run into a dead end.
AI and blockchain tech are about to change that.
Funny how it always seems to work out for humanity.
It's not different this time.

62222433
When the 19th Amendment was passed

62222442
>>62221092
It all started when the USA abolished the gold standard.

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 9 replies — 7.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-12 19:41 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62220146
I have 5 BTC in cold storage. Am I gonna make it??

62220158
>>62220146
definitely. but also because of your eth stack, with just 5 BTC no.

62221733
>>62219942
Its ok. I have monkey NFTs. They are the future

62222391
>>62219945
noisee!!! Show me your proof anon

62222489
>>62219942
I hold
0.24 BTC
6k XRP
12 LTC

AIGMI?

62222594
I'm afraid of quantum computing

item.title: There is one, exactly ONE, proven way to get rich. — 24 replies — 4.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
There is one, exactly ONE, proven way to get rich.

Open thread • Replies: 24 • Updated: 2026-05-12 15:01 UTC

OP

There is one, exactly ONE, proven way to get rich. It goes like this. Get a trade as young as possible and put 30% of your income on the S&P. Ideally more, but 30% will do it. In 20 years, you should be able to retire. In 30, you can retire rich. There you go, easy as that.

So why don't more people do this? Because they're impatient retards. They want to be rich NOW, not 20 years from now. But guess what? You don't get to be rich now, unless you're LUCKY. But you WILL be 20 years older someday, like it or not. So why not be 20 years older plus retired instead of just 20 years older?

Early replies (first 3)

62219978
Im willing to take my chances so i can retire in 5 years.

62219979
>>62219941
But I don't wanna have to think about anything but how much I enjoy doing the cummies

62219998
>>62219978
You wouldn't say this if you knew how small your chances of 'making it' in such a short period of time are (probably under 5%), or if you cared more about your future self's suffering.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62220030
>>62219998
Nice try, pussy.

62220367
>>62220030
Anon, hardly any professional traders beat the market long-term. How are you, an amateur, going to do better?

62220382
>>62220367
I am going to make 70% per year for the next 10 years straight.

62220422
>>62220382
Please keep us updated when you inevitably get rekt kek

62220440
>>62220422
Im not using options.

62220559
>>62220367
Why do absolute midwit retards repeat this over and over and over.
If beating the market was SO IMPOSSIBLE, RICH PEOPLE WOULDNT EXIST. But they beat the market by 100s of % per year. Its not Jewish magik, literally anyone could do it, if you studied it like anything else
It actually makes me sick how many people neuter their financial potential listening to cookie cutter kike "just no brain buy ETF" advice.
Yes bro the bankers are just looking out for whats best for you,,,, lol

62220623
>>62220559
>If beating the market was SO IMPOSSIBLE, RICH PEOPLE WOULDNT EXIST.
Retard take, because:
1. You can get rich just following the market, not beating it (actually very dumb of you not to get this one)
2. Most rich people don't even become rich from speculative investing
3. If there are people who can beat the market by "100s of % per year" (lol) why are there no trillionaires yet? Why aren't the richest people in the world seeing those types of returns, who is?

You genuinely just have to be kind of a dumb chud to not get this stuff intuitively

62220750
>>62220559
>If beating the market was SO IMPOSSIBLE, RICH PEOPLE WOULDNT EXIST.

Because it's not possible on a RISK-ADJUSTED BASIS, fucktard.

62220756
>>62220559
Don't bother anon, these people might retire is 40 years with 2 million (equivalent to 500k now).

62220762
>>62220756
You are an impatient gambler who will get rekt because you think real life works according to isekai progression fantasy rules.

62220773
>>62220762
Currently I am making money and you are malding. Don't worry, one day I'm suuuure I'll lose it all.

xD

62220779
>>62219941
False

Nobody gets rich investing in stocks or anything else

A successful business owner makes more in a year than you will in a lifetime and they're not buying stocks like you retards

62220791
You're right OP. Playing the stock market is gambling. That's why I'm diversified. Even the S&P 500 vanguard ETF (VOO) with njudea is too risky. I have 40% of my assets in it, 40% in vanguard value ETF(VTV) and 20% in Vanguard total international stock ETF.

IM NOT IN A RUSH I GOT MY LIFE AHEEERAD OF MEEEEE!!!

62220803
>>62220750
>the risk is determined by jews in banking who want your money
>they totally arent making high risk moves with your money, trust me this is the BEST option for YOU!

62220817
>>62220779
>A successful business owner makes more in a year than you will in a lifetime

Key word: SUCCESSFUL.

The vast majority, the VAST majority, simply fail.

>Nobody gets rich investing in stocks or anything else

Yes they do, you are fucking retarded. The easiest way to get rich is literally just to go buy pieces of businesses that are doing incredibly well, which is what you're doing when you 'buy the market'. It's the only way to get rich with near-certainty. There is no other path, I'm sorry it's not fast enough for you.

62220825
>>62220817
>The only way to get rich is not to gamble quickly but to gamble slowly over 40 years

This is for you.

62220827
>>62220817
>the VAST majority, simply fail.
All of them fail repeatedly

A successful business owner has failed dozens of times

You're too stupid to understand this. Success comes from learning. You're too lazy to ever make it

62220851
>>62220827
>A successful business owner has failed dozens of times

Lol most such people are not Pieter Levels. You are describing a skill that only a tiny percent of the population has. Notice I mentioned tradies in my OP. Why? Because the vast majority of people can't do well enough in school to go to an Ivy and become a quant and get rich that way, even if that IS a path a small number of people can take. My advice is for virtually everyone.

62220863
>>62220851
Most business owners are tradies you fucking tard

They don't get rich working a trade
They get rich owning a successful trade company

Usually after failing 3 or more times in the exact same business

62220997
>>62219941
Wrong, you could be the 10% that x10 their 1k (life changing money) then be that 10% that x10 that life changing money and then x10 again

62221049
>>62219941
>So why don't more people do this?
Because if everyone did it, it would cause a recession. Also you're making money BECAUSE people aren't doing it since we live in a consumerist economy and you are investing in the producers.

So the machine needs everyone to fall for commericals, buy expensive Mother's Day gifts, spend an absurd amount on unhealthy food, and FOMO over cars and homes while raising your expensive brat. Also they technically do under 401ks and pensions.

That's part of the game.

I support it too simply because at the end of the day, value is decided by the consumer. "Overspending on your wedding and brat" might seem pathetic to me when you can save up, but to someone else that wedding day and their child really just mean that much to them. So it works as long as you just spend money on things that actually will make you happy rather than fomoing over memecoins and your neighbor's TV. I mean I literally just paid an absurd amount on mother's Day gifts, but the catch is that the "gifts" are really just getting her to try out some stores for me at the same time so I win, she wins and the store wins lol
